text:
Once upon a time, in the land of Whiskeria, there lived a group of tiny talking owls who loved nothing more than playing pranks on the chameleonydon hedgehogs. On a bright summer day, the owls decided they wanted to organize a big picnic for all their friends, the hedgehogs. They gathered in their favorite pinewood grove, where the blueberries were ripest and sweetest.

As they were arranging plates of strawberries, raspberries, owlberry bread, and a platter of wild honeycomb, Serenia the wise owl fluttered down on her magnificent feathered wings. She had seen many picnics in her many years.

"Slysters," she hooted, wagging a great wooden toe, "the honeycomb looks especially delicate today. Careful!"

The owls chuckled softly. They knew Serenia very well.

"Of course," said Luna the lark. "We always protect our treats."
